channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Myanmar's earthquake death toll jumps to more than 1,000 as more bodies recovered from the rubble
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The death toll from a powerful 7.7 magnitude earthquake in Myanmar jumped to more than 1,000 on Saturday as more bodies were pulled from the rubble of the scores of buildings that collapsed when it struck near the country's second-largest city.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING and JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: New US strikes against Houthi rebels kill at least 1 in Yemen
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes pounded Yemen overnight into Saturday, reportedly killing at least one person as the American military acknowledged earlier bombing a major military site in the heart of Sanaa controlled by the Houthi rebels.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel strikes Beirut for the first time since a ceasefire ended the latest Israel-Hezbollah war
item.description: Israel has launched an attack on Beirut, marking its first strike on Lebanon's capital since a ceasefire ended the latest Israel-Hezbollah war last November.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Drought, wind, and debris from recent hurricanes are stoking fires across the US
item.description: Some 14,800 wildfires have burned 1,105 square miles so far this year — well above the 10-year average.
item.dc:creator: Tammy Webber, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Denmark in Trump's crosshairs as Vance makes ambitious appeal to Greenland
item.description: Vice President Vance looked to appeal to Greenland by urging it to side with the US, not Russia or China, after it seeks independence from Denmark.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel strikes Beirut for the first time since a ceasefire ended the latest Israel-Hezbollah war
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Israel on Friday launched an attack on Lebanon's capital for the first time since a ceasefire ended the latest Israel-Hezbollah war in November.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Vance slams Denmark's handling of Greenland security
item.description: Vice President Vance on Friday slammed Denmark as failing to prioritize Greenland’s security, escalating rhetoric between Washington and Copenhagen amid President Trump’s claims to take over the island.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: UN chief: World leaders must speak with one voice to prevent a new civil war in South Sudan
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The United Nations chief urged regional and international leaders Friday to prevent South Sudan from falling “over the abyss” into another civil war and speak with one voice to support a return to peace.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump administration moves to eliminate USAID, firing remaining employees
item.description: The Trump administration is moving to formally end the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID), notifying the remaining employees they will be terminated and the agency will be merged with the State Department.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Putin suggests putting Ukraine under UN-sponsored external governance
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin proposed Friday to temporarily put Ukraine under external governance as part of efforts to reach a peaceful settlement, in remarks that reflected the Kremlin leader's determination to achieve his war goals.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US, Japan, Philippines stage navy drills in disputed South China Sea as a Chinese ship keeps watch
item.description: ABOARD BRP JOSE RIZAL, South China Sea (AP) — The United States, Japan and the Philippines on Friday staged joint naval drills to boost crisis readiness off a disputed South China Sea shoal as a Chinese military ship kept watch from a distance.
item.dc:creator: AARON FAVILA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Myanmar at a glance: Embroiled in civil war, now facing more devastation after powerful earthquake
item.description: Even before a powerful 7.7-magnitude quake hit Myanmar on Friday, more than 3 million people in the country had been displaced, and hundreds of thousands were cut off from vital food and health programs as a result of a destructive four-year civil war that international groups claim has indiscrimina
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: McConnell: US headed for ‘Russia wins, America loses’ headline on Ukraine deal
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) warned in a speech Thursday that U.S. negotiators trying to end the war in Ukraine are headed for a “Russia wins, America loses” headline if they agree to a deal that achieves only “illusory peace.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, The Hill

item.title: Trump holds 'productive call' with new Canadian prime minister
item.description: President Trump spoke with Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney on Friday for the first time since Carney was elected earlier in the month amid tensions between the two nations.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Trump tariffs on autos, parts imports could add $4,711 to vehicle cost: Economist
item.description: Noted economist Arthur Laffer warns that President Trump's proposed 25% tariff on foreign-made vehicle imports could lead to a dramatic increase in prices and threaten the U.S. auto industry.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Putin says Trump plans for Greenland 'has nothing to do' with Russia
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin says President Donald Trump's ambitions for Greenland has "nothing to do" with Russia as Vice President JD Vance lands to assess security base.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Suspected US strikes pummel Houthi-controlled areas of Yemen
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes pummeled sites across Yemen controlled by the Houthi rebels early Friday, including neighborhoods in the capital, Sanaa.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Lithuanian, Polish troops aid in 'search and recovery' for 4 missing US soldiers from Fort Stewart
item.description: A Lithuanian official told Fox News Digital about ongoing search efforts to recover four missing U.S. Army soldiers in coordination with Poland.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: King Charles III briefly hospitalized for observation following side effects from cancer treatment
item.description: LONDON (AP) — King Charles III was briefly hospitalized for observation on Thursday after experiencing “temporary side effects’’ related to a scheduled cancer treatment, Buckingham Palace said in a statement. His engagements for Thursday afternoon and Friday were canceled.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Knife-wielding man injures multiple people in Amsterdam, including 2 Americans
item.description: Dutch police are searching for a motive after five people, including two Americans, were stabbed in a busy shopping area of Amsterdam on Thursday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: An apartheid-era song Elon Musk has decried as anti-white is headline news again in South Africa
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — An old anti-apartheid song that was a call for Black people to stand up against oppression and has now been decried by Elon Musk and others as promoting the killing of whites was back in the headlines in South Africa and the subject of an order by its highest
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A stabbing attack in Amsterdam wounds five people, including four foreigners
item.description: AMSTERDAM (AP) — A knife-wielding assailant in Amsterdam seriously wounded five people — including two from the United States, one from Belgium and one from Poland — in a stabbing attack Thursday on a busy shopping street, Dutch police said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: French president says not all European allies agree on a proposed force for Ukraine
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France and Britain will continue to forge ahead with plans to deploy troops in Ukraine to defend an eventual peace deal with Russia but only some other nations want to take part, French President Emmanuel Macron said Thursday after a summit of countries that have been mulling the propos
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, SAMUEL PETREQUIN and BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: His brothers’ keeper: Liran Berman calls for urgent action to save his brothers, Gali and Ziv, from Hamas
item.description: Gali and Ziv Berman were taken hostage on Oct. 7, 2023, and their brother, Liran, has been fighting for their freedom ever since. He says his brothers were "the light of every room" they walked into.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Human Rights Council blocks watchdog chief from criticizing UN official accused of antisemitism
item.description: The U.N. Human Rights Council blocked Hillel Neuer from finishing his remarks criticizing Special Rapporteur for the Occupied Palestinian Territories Francesca Albanese.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Palestinians protest Hamas in a rare public show of dissent in Gaza
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Thousands of Palestinians marched between the wreckage of a heavily destroyed town in northern Gaza on Wednesday in the second day of anti-war protests, with many chanting against Hamas in a rare display of public anger against the militant group.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, FATMA KHALED and SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea’s centuries-old Gounsa temple is left in ruins by unprecedented wildfires
item.description: Wildfires in South Korea's southern regions have left 24 people dead and destroyed more than 300 structures, including large parts of an ancient Buddhist temple complex.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sudanese army surrounds Khartoum airport in battle for capital, military sources say
item.description: The Sudanese army is encircling Khartoum airport as it battles to oust its rival Rapid Support Forces from its final foothold in Sudan's capital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Atlantic releases full Signal text chain
item.description: The Atlantic editor-in-chief on Wednesday released the full text message chain between Trump administration officials.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: US goes international for eggs amid bird flu outbreak
item.description: Imported eggs, typically used for pet food, are now being used in processed foods like cake mixes and salad dressings.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Prann, NewsNation

item.title: Gaza's Health Ministry says Israel has struck the largest hospital in the territory's south
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel’s military struck the largest hospital in southern Gaza on Sunday night, killing two people, wounding others and causing a large fire, the territory's Health Ministry said.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A Russian drone attack kills 7 in Ukraine ahead of ceasefire talks
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia launched a barrage of drones across Ukraine overnight Sunday that killed at least seven people, according to local Ukrainian officials and emergency services.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: America's European allies try to pry unspent money back from USAID
item.description: Three European allies provided millions of dollars that the United States was supposed to spend for low-income countries.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, JAMEY KEATEN, MIKE CORDER AND VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Oleg Gordievsky, Britain's most valuable Cold War spy inside the KGB, dies at 86
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Oleg Gordievsky, a Soviet KGB officer who helped change the course of the Cold War by covertly passing secrets to Britain, has died. He was 86. Gordievsky died March 4 in England, where he had lived since defecting in 1985.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis to be released from hospital Sunday after 5 weeks fighting life-threatening pneumonia
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis will be released from the hospital on Sunday after 38 days battling a severe case of pneumonia in both lungs that threatened his life on two occasions and raised the prospect — for now put to rest — of a papal resignation or funeral.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, TRISHA THOMAS and GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 2 convicted in US of Iran-backed plot to kill journalist critical of Tehran
item.description: Prosecutors said Iran’s elite Revolutionary Guard Corps paid Amirov and Omarov $500,000 for the botched hit.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation
